solution_summary: Plan to fix booking baggage pricing by correcting the long-haul classification helper and preserving explicit-price precedence. The change is localized to the booking service and validated with targeted booking-service tests covering both sides of the 6-hour threshold and explicit override behavior.
implementation_steps: Update the baggage pricing decision path in `backend/app/services/booking_service.py` so the default checked-bag fee uses the correct haul classification: `< 6 hours` -> short-haul fee, `>= 6 hours` -> long-haul fee.
Inspect `_is_long_haul` and ensure it returns `True` only when duration is at least 6 hours, without introducing a second inversion in the caller.
Keep the explicit checked-bag price path ahead of any default haul-based fee selection so user-provided prices still win.

Add or adjust service-level tests in `backend/tests/services/test_booking_service.py` for: a 3-hour flight with no explicit price, a 6-hour flight with no explicit price, and a checked bag with an explicit price on either haul type.
Run the targeted booking-service test subset first, then broader backend tests if needed to confirm no collateral impact.

planned_changes.change_summary: Correct the checked-bag default pricing branch and verify `_is_long_haul` threshold logic uses `duration >= 6 hours` as long-haul.
planned_changes.rationale: This is the root cause named in the issue; fixing it here ensures pricing aligns with the intended short/long-haul boundary.

planned_changes.change_summary: Add or update tests for short-haul default pricing, long-haul default pricing, and explicit-price override behavior.
planned_changes.rationale: Regression coverage is needed to lock in the corrected threshold logic and ensure explicit prices still bypass default pricing.

patch_strategy: Make a minimal, localized logic correction in the booking service rather than broad pricing refactors. First, confirm whether `_is_long_haul` or its caller is inverted, then change only one side so the helper/caller contract remains consistent. Preserve the explicit-price branch as the first decision point. Add focused tests that exercise the 6-hour boundary and explicit override precedence, using existing booking-service test patterns to minimize churn. Keep the patch limited to the selected files unless test failures reveal a hidden dependency.
validation_steps: Run targeted tests for booking pricing logic: `backend/tests/services/test_booking_service.py` or the narrowest test selection available.
Verify a 3-hour flight with no explicit checked-bag price resolves to 35.00.
Verify a 6-hour flight with no explicit checked-bag price resolves to 70.00.
Verify an explicitly provided checked-bag price is used regardless of flight duration.
If any helper-level tests exist for haul classification, run them as well to confirm the boundary behavior remains consistent.
pr_title: SDLC-73: Fix inverted checked-baggage haul pricing
pr_body:
## Summary
Fix the checked-baggage pricing logic in `booking_service` so short-haul flights (< 6 hours) use the short-haul default fee (€35) and long-haul flights (>= 6 hours) use the long-haul default fee (€70).

## Planned changes
- Correct the haul classification / default fee selection in `backend/app/services/booking_service.py`
- Add regression coverage in `backend/tests/services/test_booking_service.py`
- Preserve explicit checked-bag prices as the highest-priority override

## Validation
- Targeted booking-service tests for 3-hour and 6-hour boundary cases
- Explicit-price override test

## Notes
This is a localized backend pricing fix with no expected frontend impact.
risks: If `_is_long_haul` is used in other pricing paths, changing the helper could affect unrelated fee logic.
Boundary assumptions around exactly 6 hours must match existing datetime/duration semantics used by the service.
Existing tests may encode the inverted behavior and will need to be updated carefully rather than duplicated.
assumptions: The booking service is the only place where checked-baggage default haul-based pricing is decided.
Explicit checked-bag price handling already exists and should remain unchanged except for test coverage.
The repo’s current test suite includes or can easily accommodate service-level tests for booking pricing.
